methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Silicon Quantum Computing, founded by Michelle Simmons, is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised A$83M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a limited but growing peer universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Peer-median valuation analysis was not applicable in this instance. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers overall, but filtering by stage (Series A), adjacent stage, and valuation tier yielded fewer than the three comparable datapoints required for robust median analysis. A broader sector median would introduce material heterogeneity across development stages and business models—from early-stage research platforms to commercialized quantum-as-a-service offerings—and therefore would not be sufficiently reliable.

This analysis cannot arrive at a triangulated valuation range without applicable quantitative methods. Forward progression would require either (1) expansion of the comparable set through broader stage inclusion with explicit stage-risk adjustments, (2) precedent transaction analysis if M&A or later-round data becomes available, or (3) fundamental revenue/margin projections paired with sector-specific multiples once the company approaches revenue generation. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient same-stage, narrow-peer comparable transactions to anchor valuation.
